note: Per-game custom VFX generation. Complements vfx_library (20 pre-rendered canonical effects) — effect_custom lets a scaffold produce title-specific variants that match the game's palette + pixel-art era. 5 sub_kinds per sister's extraction taxonomy.
_data_driven_design: Based on corpus analysis (99 effect_layer animations, 25 essences): sub_kinds distribute as explosion_vfx (22), spell_vfx (14), aura_vfx (12), atmospheric_vfx (4), summon_vfx (1). Bimodal canvas sizes: 16×16 hit-sparks vs 128×128 explosions. Most are 4-frame horizontal strips; aura_vfx tend to be 8-frame loops.

explosion_vfx.description: Destruction burst — death, bomb, crash, boss-destruction. High-contrast, short-lived.
explosion_vfx.notes: Small-variant is 16×16 impact-spark; large-variant is 128×128 death-explosion. Scaffolds pick per-use-case.

spell_vfx.description: Magic cast animation — fire / ice / heal / thunder / summon. Multi-frame arc or sustained glow.
spell_vfx.notes: JRPG spell canonical. 8 frames covers a full cast arc (gather → peak → dissipate). Dragon Quest is the dominant reference essence.

aura_vfx.description: Persistent glow / shimmer / charge — buff / invulnerability / power-up-active.
aura_vfx.notes: Unlike explosion/spell (fire-and-forget), aura loops while the status is active. Screen-blend is more common here than alpha-over.

atmospheric_vfx.description: Ambient scene-level effect — rain / fog / sparks / ember-drift / dust-motes.
atmospheric_vfx.notes: Scene-attached, not entity-attached. Tiled + alpha-feathered at scene edges for seamless multi-region coverage.

summon_vfx.description: Summon/teleport/warp portal — enemy spawn, boss-appear, warp-in, summon-creature.
summon_vfx.notes: Longer than explosion (gather-phase + emergence). Final Fantasy summon-in canonical. One essence currently (`1991_final_fantasy_iv`); backfill targets listed in SPRITE_SUBKIND_ESSENCE_MAP.md sole-contributor warnings.

palette_presets.fire_orange: orange-red flames with yellow core — classic explosion palette
palette_presets.ice_cyan: cyan-white crystalline shards — ice magic / freeze effects
palette_presets.electric_yellow: bright yellow zig-zag with white core — thunder / lightning magic
palette_presets.holy_white: pure white with gold highlights + radial rays — heal / divine magic
palette_presets.dark_purple: deep purple with black core — necromancy / summon / curse
palette_presets.green_plasma: translucent green particle glow — poison / alien / nature magic
palette_presets.blood_red: dark red with sharp edges — gore / critical-hit / death
palette_presets.smoke_grey: grey puff cloud, wispy — teleport / smoke-bomb / atmospheric haze
palette_presets.water_blue: blue translucent ripple — water magic / splash
palette_presets.ember_float: small orange points drifting upward — ember-drift atmospheric

implementation_notes: Identity preservation is NOT required — each effect call is standalone. Seed is for reproducibility.
Mode='icon' (magenta chromakey → transparent RGBA) for all effect sub_kinds. Effects need alpha for compositing.
Explosion bimodal sizes: 16×16 hit-spark (small) vs 128×128 death-explosion (large). Scaffolds pick via `cell_size_px` param at call-time.

Aura_vfx is the one sub_kind that NEEDS to loop seamlessly — end-frame should match start-frame. Prompt the template with 'loops back to frame 1' instruction; postprocess verifies via pixel-diff between frame[0] and frame[-1].
Atmospheric tiled effects (rain/fog) need horizontal tileability — same feather-edge pass as parallax_backdrop workflow.
Runtime handles rotation for atmospheric direction variance (rain angle, dust drift). Single-direction generation + runtime-transform saves ERNIE calls.
